title: Rhea Seehorn Wins Golden Globe for Best Actress in TV Drama
text: Rhea Seehorn claimed the Golden Globe for best actress in a TV drama for her role as Carol Sturka in Apple TV's "Pluribus." This marks her inaugural nomination and victory in this category. Upon accepting the award, Seehorn appeared visibly moved. She humorously referenced her prepared remarks, saying: "My speech says, 'Get a prescription for beta blockers,' but I did not. Sorry!" The actress expressed gratitude to presenter Queen Latifah, recalling a meaningful encounter from two decades prior: "Queen Latifah, I met you about 20 years ago between jobs at a Koo Koo Roo and you were so nice, so thanks!" Seehorn acknowledged each competing nominee by name and praised their work. She also expressed appreciation for her female colleagues in attendance, noting they had been "incredible in the last couple of weeks." She concluded by thanking "Pluribus" creator Vince Gilligan and her collaborators, stating: "I am still so grateful that I get to do this for a living, and this is incredible icing on that cake." Seehorn is widely recognized for portraying Kim Wexler in "Better Call Saul," earning multiple Emmy and Critics' Choice nominations. For "Pluribus," she previously won the Critics' Choice Award for best drama series actress.
